summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Apple Plans Siri AI Upgrade To Outpace Perplexity As Wall Street Pushes $30 Billion Buyout: Report
summary: Apple is developing an AI-powered search tool called "World Knowledge Answers" for Siri, signaling a shift toward in-house AI innovation over acquisitions.

headline: Nvidia's Jensen Huang Mentored SoundHound AI Founder For A Decade, Helping Turn A Dorm-Room Idea Into $5.4 Billion Voice Tech Powerhouse
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang's decade-long mentorship helped SoundHound AI founder Keyvan Mohajer grow a dorm-room idea into a $5.8 billion voice tech company now gaining Wall Street momentum.

headline: Palantir CEO Alex Karp Once Pushed For 'Department Of Offense' — Now Trump Rebrands Pentagon To 'War' Office As PLTR Touts America's AI Edge
summary: President Donald Trump rebranded the Pentagon as the "Department of War" on Friday, echoing Palantir CEO Alex Karp's earlier call for a more offensive U.S. military strategy as AI becomes central to global power competition.

headline: Apple Faces Class-Action Lawsuit Over Reported Use Of Copyrighted Books To Train AI
summary: Apple faces a lawsuit for allegedly using copyrighted books without permission to train its OpenELM AI model.

headline: Trump's Nvidia Export Deal Collides With AI GAIN Act — Chipmaker Warns New Bill Could Cripple Global Competition
summary: Nvidia warned that a new U.S. bill could hurt its global edge by restricting AI chip exports, while growing pressure pushes tech firms like OpenAI to seek alternatives.

headline: Jeff Bezos-Backed Anthropic To Pay $1.5 Billion In AI Copyright Settlement With Authors After $13 Billion Funding Round
summary: Anthropic, backed by Amazon and Google, agreed to a $1.5B settlement with authors over using pirated books to train its AI, marking a historic first in the AI copyright battle.
